name: cc-harness
description: cc-harness — a layered harness for Claude Code: cc-config (the front desk — /cc-init onboarding, recommended rules, the cheat-sheet hook) sets it up; cc-tools (philosophy-agnostic primitives: slap, likec4, reminder, cctreectl) + cc-pipeline (the ground -> diverge -> decide -> build product script) are what you drive; cc-worker (the second pilot — skill + native subagent) runs them; cc-chief (the top brain) orchestrates workers across the product's sub-projects.

plugins.name: cc-config
plugins.description: The front desk: install, configure, and maintain the harness itself. Commands only — config is the human's to run; the model can't invoke it. /cc-init is the onboarding orchestrator — it explains the layers, installs missing dependencies and recommended tools, then runs three setup commands behind skip/stop gates: /rules-management (recommended + project rules), /cheatsheet-management (the reminder cheat-sheet a hook re-surfaces), /docs-management (find stale prose). Ships the recommended project rules and the cheat-sheet hook. Install this first.

plugins.name: cc-tools
plugins.description: The primitives layer: philosophy-agnostic skills you reach for while working, usable in any project, invokable by the model or by you — /slap resets a stuck fix, /likec4 draws C4 architecture-as-code, /reminder pins one rule file as a hard constraint, and cctreectl maps the project tree. Skills only, zero-config.

plugins.name: cc-pipeline
plugins.description: The product script: six stage skills, no commands (invoke each by slash or let the model route to it). /planner grounds the North Star and charts a roadmap, /architect is the architecture gateway (routes to architect-design or architect-reflect, one invariant: only architecturally-significant pieces in the LikeC4 model), /what-to-do ranks where to go next, /how-to-do decides one fork, /do builds one task to a smoke-checked finish, /refactor-review-test hardens it and commits — a ground -> diverge -> decide -> build pipeline. Depends on cc-tools.

plugins.name: cc-worker
plugins.description: The project's second pilot: hand /worker any piece of work and it adapts — reading the goal (roadmap.md) and design (architecture) and reaching for the right cc-pipeline and cc-tools skills to carry it out. Ships as both a skill (invoke /worker, or the model routes to it) and a native subagent (delegate a whole task to a worker in its own context). Three invariants: keeps the roadmap true, changes architecture only through architect first, obeys the flags (--auto/--fast/--worktree/--ultracode). Depends on cc-pipeline and cc-tools.

plugins.name: cc-chief
plugins.description: The top brain: the /chief skill orchestrates the workers across a product's sub-projects (each a git repo under the current folder). Two responsibilities — keep each sub-project's roadmap true (via its cc-pipeline:planner), and orchestrate the workers: reason about cross-repo dependencies, sequence provider-first, and dispatch workers as native subagents selectively. It conducts; the workers build. Depends on cc-worker.
